summary: Screened 80 source items. Top 12 candidates emphasize agent security, sandboxing, persistent memory, MCP tooling, and runtime control. Leading projects: GoClaw, Flowork_Agent, Prismor, yoloai, agentguard, duduclaw, tokenfuse, perseus-vault, forcefield, phinq, ai-agent-nix-sandboxing, rocketride-server.

candidates.title: GoClaw: Multi-tenant AI agent deployment with isolation
candidates.why_it_matters: Enables secure team-scale agent deployment with 5-layer security and native concurrency.

candidates.title: Flowork_Agent: Self-hosted agents with per-agent brain
candidates.why_it_matters: Provides isolated persistent memory, immune system, and MCP orchestration in a single binary.

candidates.title: Prismor: Runtime security for AI agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Blocks dangerous commands, secret leaks, and prompt injection in real time.

candidates.title: yoloai: AI agent sandboxing done right
candidates.why_it_matters: Lightweight sandboxing for isolating AI agent execution environments.

candidates.title: agentguard: AgentOps security scanner
candidates.why_it_matters: Scans MCP configs, transcripts, and PR diffs for agent security issues.

candidates.title: duduclaw: Multi-LLM agent platform with 80+ MCP tools
candidates.why_it_matters: Unifies Claude, Codex, Gemini with agent routing and extensive MCP ecosystem.

candidates.title: tokenfuse: Runtime cost control for agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Adds per-run budgets, loop detection, and kill-switch to prevent runaway costs.

candidates.title: perseus-vault: Persistent memory MCP server
candidates.why_it_matters: Local-first SQLite + vector memory with encryption and bi-temporal history.

candidates.title: forcefield: Security guardrails for AI coding agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Monitors and blocks dangerous files, commands, and prompt injection in real time.

candidates.title: phinq: Runtime checkpoint with human-in-the-loop
candidates.why_it_matters: Classifies tool calls by risk before execution, enabling safe agent operation.

candidates.title: ai-agent-nix-sandboxing: Linux namespace sandbox for agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Sandboxes coding agents using user namespaces without host privileges.

candidates.title: rocketride-server: High-performance AI pipeline with agent orchestration
candidates.why_it_matters: C++ core pipeline engine supporting 50+ nodes, multi-provider LLMs, and agent workflows.

gaps: Dedicated agent evaluation frameworks are sparse; most security tools focus on runtime blocking rather than testing.
Storage solutions for agent memory are primarily SQLite-based; no dedicated agent storage infrastructure.
Deployment-specific tooling is limited to rocketride-server's pipeline orchestration.
